---
title: "Lab 14 – ESP-NOW Communication"
canvas:
  type: assignment
  published: false
  points: 1
  grading_type: pass_fail
  submission_types: [online_upload]
  allowed_extensions: [pdf, png, jpg, jpeg, mp4, mov]
---

## Learning Goals

- Establish wireless communication between remote (XIAO) and car (ESP32 DevKit)
- Understand ESP-NOW: peer pairing, MAC addresses, data packets
- Integrate with motor and servo control

## Background

**ESP-NOW** is a fast, connectionless wireless protocol. No router needed — devices communicate directly via MAC addresses. Latency ~1–2ms, range ~30–50m, payload up to 250 bytes.

## Tasks

1. Get MAC addresses of both the XIAO and the DevKit.
2. Upload **transmitter** code to the XIAO:

```cpp
#include <esp_now.h>
#include <WiFi.h>

// Replace with your DevKit's MAC address
uint8_t receiverMAC[] = {0xAA, 0xBB, 0xCC, 0xDD, 0xEE, 0xFF};

typedef struct {
  int16_t steering;   // -255 to 255
  int16_t throttle;   // -255 to 255
  bool button;
} ControlData;

ControlData data;
esp_now_peer_info_t peerInfo;

void setup() {
  Serial.begin(115200);
  WiFi.mode(WIFI_STA);
  esp_now_init();
  memcpy(peerInfo.peer_addr, receiverMAC, 6);
  peerInfo.channel = 0;
  peerInfo.encrypt = false;
  esp_now_add_peer(&peerInfo);
}

void loop() {
  data.steering = map(analogRead(A0), 0, 4095, -255, 255);
  data.throttle = map(analogRead(A1), 0, 4095, -255, 255);
  data.button = !digitalRead(D2);
  esp_now_send(receiverMAC, (uint8_t *)&data, sizeof(data));
  delay(20);
}
```

3. Upload **receiver** code to the ESP32 DevKit:

```cpp
#include <esp_now.h>
#include <WiFi.h>

typedef struct {
  int16_t steering;
  int16_t throttle;
  bool button;
} ControlData;

ControlData receivedData;

void onReceive(const esp_now_recv_info_t *info,
               const uint8_t *data, int len) {
  memcpy(&receivedData, data, sizeof(receivedData));
  Serial.print("Steering: ");
  Serial.print(receivedData.steering);
  Serial.print(" Throttle: ");
  Serial.print(receivedData.throttle);
  Serial.print(" Btn: ");
  Serial.println(receivedData.button);
}

void setup() {
  Serial.begin(115200);
  WiFi.mode(WIFI_STA);
  esp_now_init();
  esp_now_register_recv_cb(onReceive);
}

void loop() { delay(10); }
```

4. Verify joystick values appear on the DevKit's Serial Monitor.
5. Test range — walk away with the remote. At what distance do you lose signal?
6. **Integrate:** Use received steering to control the servo (Lab 11) and throttle to control the BTS7960 (Lab 10).

::: {.callout-important}
**Failsafe:** What should the car do if no data is received for 500ms? Implement a timeout that stops the motor!
:::

## Submission

Upload a **video** showing the remote controlling the motor and/or servo wirelessly, or a photo of the Serial Monitor showing received data.
